2606.29897.title: Child-Centric Voice Anonymization in Single and Multi-Speaker Speech via Domain-Adapted SSL Models
2606.29897.paper_summary: Voice anonymization aims to protect speaker identity while preserving linguistic content and speech usability. However, most anonymization systems are developed on adult speech, leading to degraded performance when applied to child speech. This paper investigates child-centric anonymization by adapting a self-supervised learning (SSL) based anonymization pipeline to the child speech domain. The system is adapted using child speech from the MyST corpus and evaluated under both single-speaker and two-speaker mixture conditions. Experimental results show that child-domain adaptation improves intelligibility and perceptual quality while maintaining strong privacy protection. Extending the approach to multi-speaker further demonstrates that combining target speaker extraction with child-adapted anonymization provides privacy protection while preserving conversational structure. These findings highlight the importance of child-specific adaptation for practical speech anonymization systems.
2606.29897.paper_summary_zh: 語音匿名化旨在保護說話者的身份，同時保留語言內容和語音可用性。<lb/>然而，大多數匿名化系統是基於成人語音開發的，這導致在應用於兒童語音時性能下降。<lb/>本文通過將自我監督學習（SSL）基於的匿名化流程調整到兒童語音領域，研究了以兒童為中心的匿名化。<lb/>該系統使用來自MyST語料庫的兒童語音進行調整，並在單一說話者和雙說話者混合條件下進行評估。<lb/>實驗結果顯示，兒童領域的適應提高了可理解性和感知質量，同時保持了強大的隱私保護。<lb/>將該方法擴展到多說話者進一步證明，將目標說話者提取與兒童適應的匿名化相結合，提供了隱私保護，同時保留了對話結構。<lb/>這些發現突顯了為實用語音匿名化系統進行兒童特定適應的重要性。
2606.29897.author: Pranav Tushar et.al.
2606.29897.authors: Pranav Tushar, Xiao Xiao Miao, Rong Tong
